config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before individual rights, because that opens the door to abuse by governments or majorities. In my view, the best societies protect basic rights first and then pursue the common good through fair laws and mutual responsibility. From my perspective, collective goals are important, but they should not erase personal dignity and freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, pengalaman sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” hak minoritas dan kebebasan pribadi mudah dikorbankan. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang membatasi kekuasaan kolektif dengan hak-hak dasar yang tidak bisa dilanggar semaunya. Bagi saya, prioritas yang lebih aman dan adil adalah melindungi hak individu terlebih dahulu, lalu mengejar tujuan bersama lewat aturan yang transparan dan terbatas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically take priority over individual rights. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of harmony and the common good, but I have also seen how easily “for society” can become a justification for silencing people, especially when power is uneven. A healthy society is not built by sacrificing basic rights first; it is built by protecting those rights and then asking people to contribute responsibly to the collective good. If society can override individuals whenever it wants, then the strongest voices, not the fairest ones, will decide what “the common good” means.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, terutama terhadap minoritas atau orang yang tidak populer. Kepentingan masyarakat itu penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang membatasi kekuasaan kolektif dengan hak dasar yang tidak bisa dilanggar sembarangan. Menurut saya, urutannya harus kebalikan: lindungi dulu hak individu, lalu kejar tujuan bersama dengan aturan yang adil dan transparan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should normally take priority over the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “for the public good” can be used to pressure dissenters and excuse unfair treatment. Harmony matters, yes, but harmony that is built by silencing people is fake harmony, not justice. The strongest point from the other side is that society does need order and shared responsibility, but that does not require putting rights second by default. For me, basic rights should be the limit on what society can demand, not a privilege society may suspend whenever it feels convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu secara umum, karena menurut saya hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan sesuatu yang dikalahkan oleh kepentingan masyarakat kapan saja. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya cenderung curiga ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk memberi terlalu banyak kuasa pada negara atau mayoritas, karena sejarah kami penuh contoh di mana minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer dirugikan. Poin bahwa masyarakat butuh ketertiban dan tanggung jawab bersama itu benar, tetapi ketertiban tidak sama dengan membiarkan hak dasar dikesampingkan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru yang kuat karena melindungi kebebasan individu, lalu membangun kepentingan bersama di atas perlindungan itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
